# GitHub Copilot instructions for VIVIDUS

## Build & quality validation

Before committing any Java changes, run the full quality suite for every affected module:

```bash
JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/temurin-21-jdk-amd64 ./gradlew \
  :<module>:checkstyleMain :<module>:checkstyleTest \
  :<module>:spotbugsMain \
  :<module>:test
```

Fix **all** violations before pushing. The CI `build` task runs `checkstyle`, `spotbugs`, and `pmd` on Linux; failures there cause the PR to be blocked.

For the `vividus-to-xray-exporter` module specifically:

```bash
JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/temurin-21-jdk-amd64 ./gradlew \
  :vividus-to-xray-exporter:checkstyleMain :vividus-to-xray-exporter:checkstyleTest \
  :vividus-to-xray-exporter:spotbugsMain \
  :vividus-to-xray-exporter:test
```

## Files never to commit

The following files **must not** be staged or committed:

- `gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar`
- `gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties`
- Any `.gradle/` build output directory

These are either managed by the `vividus-build-system` submodule or are build artefacts. If accidentally staged, remove them with `git rm --cached`.

## Spring / Qodana patterns

### Optional as field or parameter (QDJVM S3553)

**Never** use `Optional<T>` as a field type or constructor/method parameter. Use `@org.jspecify.annotations.Nullable T` instead. Call `Optional.ofNullable(value)` only at the use site when an `Optional` return type is required by the called API.

```java
// Wrong
private Optional<String> jiraInstanceKey;

// Correct
private @Nullable String jiraInstanceKey;
```

### Conditionally absent beans (QDJVM — "Could not autowire")

When a bean is conditionally present (`@ConditionalOnProperty`, `@ConditionalOnMissingBean`, etc.), inject it with `@Lazy` in beans that are always registered, to prevent Qodana from reporting a false "could not autowire" error.

```java
@Bean
public XrayServerClient xrayServerClient(@Lazy JiraClientProvider jiraClientProvider) { ... }
```

### Spring Boot configuration metadata

SonarCloud and Qodana analyse source files, not build output, so metadata generated by `spring-boot-configuration-processor` at compile time is invisible to them. When introducing new `@ConfigurationProperties` keys that would otherwise be flagged as "cannot resolve configuration property", commit a hand-written metadata file:

```
src/main/resources/META-INF/additional-spring-configuration-metadata.json
```

## JUnit assertions and annotations

- Prefer `assertTrue(condition)` / `assertFalse(condition)` over `assertEquals(true, condition)` / `assertEquals(false, condition)`.
- Use `org.junit.jupiter.api.util.SetSystemProperty` / `ClearSystemProperty` (JUnit 6.1 native). Keep JUnit Pioneer (`@SetSystemProperty`, `@ClearSystemProperty`) only where `StdIo` or `SetEnvironmentVariable` is required.

## General code style

- **No duplicated logic.** Extract shared code into a private or protected method rather than copy-pasting.
- Prefer concise expressions; avoid unnecessary local variables.
- This project is single-threaded in most components — do not add synchronization (`synchronized`, `Lock`, `AtomicReference`) unless there is an explicit multi-threading requirement.
- All public API classes and step methods must have Javadoc.

## Documentation

Documentation pages live under `docs/modules/ROOT/pages/` in AsciiDoc format. When adding a new feature, add or update the corresponding `.adoc` file in the same PR.
